classification.domain: side-channel attacks on masked ML-KEM implementations

topic.what: Systematic evaluation of plaintext-checking oracle (PCO) attacks against masked software implementations of ML-KEM Keccak functions
topic.how: Profiled template attacks using Gaussian templates in principal subspaces (LDA) against PINI, DOM, and TI masked implementations on ARM Cortex-M4, measuring leakage from re-encryption step
topic.why_matters: Quantifies and validates the 'FO-Calypse' threat against protected implementations, showing high-security masked implementations (up to 7 shares) can be broken with only ~50 ML-KEM executions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Accuracy of message distinguishing oracle to distinguish b=0 vs b=1
characteristics.evidence: Figure 9: DOM C 7-share achieves 90% accuracy with ~10^4 Keccak rounds (≈50 ML-KEM executions)
characteristics.context: DOM C is hardest implementation; attacks scale to 7 shares

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of ML-KEM decapsulations needed for key recovery
characteristics.value: ~5x reduction with soft PCO vs hard PCO
characteristics.vs_baseline: Hard PCO requires significantly more traces
characteristics.evidence: Figure 10a: Soft PCO reduces traces by ~5x compared to hard PCO approach
characteristics.context: Soft information exploitation is more efficient than majority voting

characteristics.value: up to 2.8 TB for 6-share PINI ASM training
characteristics.evidence: Table 4: Memory footprint increases with shares; 6-share PINI ASM requires 2.8 TB training data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Keccak round execution cycles on target
characteristics.evidence: Table 2: Cycle counts increase linearly with shares; assembly implementations slower due to transition leakage prevention
characteristics.context: Faster implementations (C vs ASM) marginally harder to attack

characteristics.what_it_measures: Estimated security against lattice reduction attacks
characteristics.value: plateau at ~12 bits for high-share implementations
characteristics.evidence: Figure 10b: Lattice estimator shows plateau at ~12 bits when variance approaches 0
characteristics.context: Conservative estimates; estimators lack granularity for probabilistic side-channel hints

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of oracle queries for full key recovery
characteristics.value: Soft approach ~5x more efficient than hard
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.2: Soft Bayesian approach reduces required decapsulations by ~5x
characteristics.context: Soft information exploitation outperforms hard decisions

good_for: Comparing masking schemes (PINI vs DOM vs TI)
Designing security evaluations for PCO-based attacks
requires: Large storage for profiling datasets (>1 TB)

novelty: First systematic PCO evaluation against masked Keccak implementations up to 7 shares
Simple parametric model for PCOs targeting masked Keccak with Gaussian assumptions
Formal proof that soft PCO approaches outperform hard majority voting
Integration of lattice estimators with side-channel soft information
Demonstration that computing more (more shares) implies leaking more
